nodes.label: P2.0 — re-establish the wall + DECIDE (lost vs entangled)
nodes.description: Locked first run of Phase 2 (2026-06-21). Reproduces the deep-SCFF wall on CIFAR-10-flat (8 layers, layer-norm, sum-squared goodness, two-sided theta=2.0) and runs the DECIDE fork: max-power MLP readout on frozen SCFF features vs untrained random projection vs raw input vs pure-GD. Also measures width x depth gap (F6+) and enriches with dead-unit/effective-rank cause diagnostics.

nodes.label: P2.0 verdict: LOST (not entangled)
nodes.description: CIFAR-flat: wall slope -0.018/layer (5/5 seeds negative), probe 0.23->0.117 (chance 0.10). DECIDE: SCFF 0.294 ~= RAND 0.298 -> selectivity -0.005 (negative). Dead-units 0.02->0.47; effective rank 39->11. Cause = both axes (squared-goodness deactivation + length-norm rank-collapse). Synth control has no wall (slope ~0), confirming CIFAR is the real headline.

nodes.label: Decision: LOST -> route to P2.1 (norm x goodness grid)
nodes.description: Because SCFF features are LOST not entangled, the fix must preserve/rebuild representation, not re-interface (P2.4 deprioritized). Cause split into norm-cause (rank-collapse, Trifecta) and goodness-cause (deactivation, DeeperForward), pre-routing P2.1 to start at layer-norm+linear-goodness.
nodes.rationale: Selectivity control caught what a naive gap-vs-ceiling reading would have mis-routed as 'entangled -> interface problem'.

nodes.label: DeeperForward (ICLR 2025)
nodes.description: Argues layer-norm should be KEPT but goodness switched squared->linear/mean, because layer-norm's mean-zero property decouples linear goodness from the forward representation with no batch statistics needed. Reaches 17 layers. Counter-finding to Trifecta's batch-norm-is-the-fix premise.
